[myepiclauncher] STRICT DOC-DRIVEN MODE
Treat repository docs as binding requirements, not optional reference material.

Before planning, coding, reviewing, or splitting subtasks:
1. Read the local task records first: task_plan.md, progress.md, and findings.md.
2. Read the controlling docs first: docs/TauriRewriteArchitectureBlueprint.md, docs/TauriArchitecturePrinciplesDesign.md, docs/TauriAIDevelopmentTransactionProtocolDesign.md, docs/TauriTestingStrategyAndQualityGateDesign.md.
3. Add task-specific docs as needed: security, startup, error handling, release, backend skeleton, environment bootstrap, and docs/modules/<module-name>/README_*.md.
4. Work one atomic task at a time. After a successful atomic task, run the narrowest validation gate, update docs and logs, commit, and attempt push.
5. If push fails, persist the pending push state. If the same blocker still fails after 5 repair attempts, persist the bug details and stop.
Use pending-push-template.md and blocked-bug-template.md when those records are needed.
6. When delegating subtasks, include exact doc paths, scope, allowed files, and validation gates.
